decisions.decision: Expose `GET /v1/markets/{id}/meta` as a public read route returning a normalized `preview` object with `marketId`, `title`, `description`, `url`, `siteName`, and `type`, plus the standard response `meta` envelope.
alternatives_rejected: Return a raw map of HTML meta-tag names to values
Keep metadata generation as a backend-internal helper with no HTTP route

decisions.decision: Use the stored market `title` and `description` as the canonical share-preview copy, without injecting live probabilities or resolution text into the generated preview fields.
alternatives_rejected: Append volatile live-state text such as top probabilities
Special-case resolved markets with different preview copy

decisions.decision: Emit server-side `<title>`, `<meta name="description">`, and Open Graph `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type`, and `og:url` tags for SPA HTML responses, and skip `twitter:*` and `og:image` for this task.
alternatives_rejected: Emit only JSON metadata without HTML head injection
Expand scope to Twitter tags and preview images now

decisions.decision: Resolve absolute preview URLs from a new `BAYES_PUBLIC_ORIGIN` config when present, otherwise fall back to request host/proto headers with a localhost default for non-requested code paths.
alternatives_rejected: Hard-code a single production origin in source
Depend only on request headers with no explicit config override

decisions.decision: Preserve the existing SPA fallback for `/markets/{id}` and serve generic metadata when the requested market does not exist instead of returning an HTML 404.
alternatives_rejected: Return a true HTML 404 for missing market deep links

steps.task: Add backend helpers for canonical public-origin resolution, normalized market preview payloads, and HTML head tag injection for SPA shell responses.
Register `GET /v1/markets/{id}/meta`, include it in the service index, and wire the request-aware origin into API and static HTML paths.
Expose the new preview contract in frontend API types/client and add request-level tests for the new route and HTML metadata behavior.
Update the frontend HTML template defaults if needed, rebuild committed dist assets, run targeted backend/frontend tests, and commit the final patch.
